channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Private jets collide at Arizona Airport, killing 1 person
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The iconic ‘T’ stands for twisted in Tory Burch’s new collection at New York Fashion Week
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Tory Burch not only put her own twist on American sportswear in her new fall/winter collection at New York Fashion Week — she made it “twist-ed,” with exaggerated shapes and details, a splashy backdrop and memorable music.
item.dc:creator: BROOKE LEFFERTS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Some Israeli soldiers traveling abroad are targeted for alleged war crimes in Gaza
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — An Israeli army reservist's dream vacation in Brazil ended abruptly last month over an accusation that he committed war crimes in the Gaza Strip. Yuval Vagdani woke up on Jan.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Top Justice Department official orders prosecutors to drop charges against New York Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department on Monday ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, arguing in a remarkable departure from long-standing norms that the case was interfering with the mayor’s ability to aid the president’s crackdown on illega
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Recent aviation disasters cause fears about the safety of flying
item.description: The spate of recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington, the fiery plane crash in Philadelphia and a plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 are only the most high-profile disasters.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Labor unions sue to block DOGE access to sensitive information at US agencies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A coalition of labor unions filed a lawsuit Monday asking a federal court to stop Elon Musk’s team from accessing private data at the Education Department, the Treasury Department and the Office of Personnel Management.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Brother of Parkland shooter arrested for trespassing at 3 schools
item.description: Zachary Cruz, the 24-year-old brother of the Parkland school shooter, was arrested Feb. 4 for trespassing at multiple school properties, including Wilson Middle School, Wilson High School, and the Valley Career and Technical Center.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Sutherland, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illegal alien charged in sandwich shop owner's murder caught and released into US under Biden: ICE
item.description: An illegal alien from the Dominican Republic has been charged with the home invasion murder of a Massachusetts sandwich shop owner named Ilias "Louie" Mavros.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Catalytic converters stolen from Illinois district's school buses, forcing students to stay home
item.description: A suburban Illinois school district was forced to hold an emergency e-learning day after the catalytic converters were stolen from "over 80%" of its buses.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: Super Bowl 'flu'? Record number of employees didn't go to work Monday, poll suggests
item.description: “May be calling out sick tomorrow,” one Eagles supporter wrote on X on Sunday night. “Got that birds flu.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he’s in ‘no rush’ to develop Gaza
item.description: “Think of it as a big real estate site, and the United States is going to own it, and will slowly, very slowly, we’re in no rush, in development,” Trump told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: So long, penny! Trump orders US to ditch 1-cent coin after decades of complaints
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — The lowly penny, the forgotten mainstay of coin jars and car cupholders everywhere, may soon be no more. President Donald Trump announced Sunday he’s ordered his administration to cease production of the 1-cent coin, whose buying power is long past its prime.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of Chesapeake man missing since last July still hoping for answers
item.description: "He's been gone six months now, and it's just hard to go on without him," Cynthia Wolf, Bill Wolf's wife said.
item.dc:creator: Cheyenne Pagan, NewsNation

item.title: Protester speaks after being hit by car during Salt Lake City demonstration
item.description: Padilla-Oliver said the driver slowed down and stopped, after which she "stood with pride" and held her sign.
item.dc:creator: MJ Jewkes, NewsNation

item.title: Small Kansas town wants to pay you to move in
item.description: Applicants must make at least $50,000 annually and have a remote job where they are allowed to relocate.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday. Here’s what you need to know. Starbucks The coffee chain is offering its rewards members a free tall hot or iced brewed coffee any time during “Starbucks Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Video: Rare ‘Black Demon’ fish spotted alive for first time
item.description: The scary-looking fish is usually found in water depths of 1,500 meters, where little to no sunlight reaches.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Crowd gathers to visit Bob's Red Mill Restaurant final time
item.description: A crowd gathered outside the restaurant on Sunday, the final day of operation, to visit one last time.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Foran, NewsNation

item.title: Teachers' union heard coaching migrants evading ICE despite Homan's warning to Democrat leaders
item.description: A recently surfaced video shows a New York City teachers' union providing instructions on how to help migrant families evade Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance proves pivotal in pushing through controversial Trump nominees
item.description: Vice President Vance has quickly become President Trump's liaison to the Senate, which has paid dividends as the GOP-led body moves to confirm even Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: NICU babies in Rhode Island celebrate first Valentine's Day
item.description: "This touch of celebration brightens the day not only for families, but also for our staff," a spokesperson for Care New England said.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: California small plane crashes upside down, 2 occupants survive and refuse medical treatment
item.description: Two people inside a small plane that landed upside down in a California vegetable field refused medical treatment and were able to walk away from the crash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A 7.6 magnitude earthquake shakes the Caribbean, some islands urge residents to leave coastlines
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A magnitude-7.6 earthquake shook the Caribbean Sea south of the Cayman Islands Saturday, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. Several islands and countries urged people near the coastline to move inland but authorities in most places later lifted the tsunami alerts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Philadelphia radio station bans Taylor Swift music ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: A release from the station claims that 94.5 is "pressing pause on all things Taylor Swift." The announcement came after a Swift fan and a Philly advertising agency spent the week trolling each other with ads in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, Fox 5

item.title: The US stopped allowing passport gender marker changes. Here are some of the people affected
item.description: The day after President Donald Trump returned to office, Lisa Suhay took her 21-year-old daughter, Mellow, to a passport office in Norfolk, Virginia, where they live. Getting a passport for Mellow, who is transgender, was urgent.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, KRON4

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration orders federal agencies to provide lists of underperforming employees
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has ordered all federal departments and agencies to provide lists of employees who are underperforming, as it seeks to shrink the workforce and awaits a court ruling related to its deferred resignation offers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, WGN 9

item.title: Baseball star Shohei Ohtani’s ex-interpreter is sentenced to nearly 5 years in sports betting case
item.description: SANTA ANA, Calif. (AP) — The former interpreter for baseball star Shohei Ohtani was sentenced Thursday to nearly five years in prison for bank and tax fraud after he stole nearly $17 million from the Los Angeles Dodgers player’s bank account.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New Mexico courtroom devolves into brawl as 3 people rush defendant in homicide case
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A hearing in a New Mexico courtroom devolved into chaos when three people rushed the defendant in a homicide case, setting off a brawl with flying fists and kicks. Courtroom video cameras recorded the Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: Texas authorities arrested a Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by President Trump on Thursday for online solicitation of a minor.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5
